title: 17 arrested during St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seventeen individuals were arrested at South Boston's St. Patrick's Day parade, as police targeted public drinking activities.
The parade's route changed, starting at Andrew Square to improve crowd flow and honor the 250th Evacuation Day.
Residents reported issues like underage drinking and public indecency, leading to multiple 311 complaints during the event.

title: 2026 Oscars: Full list of winners (updating live)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress for 'Weapons' at the 2026 Oscars on March 15.
'KPop Demon Hunters' won Best Animated Feature at the 2026 Oscars.
A rare tie occurred in Best Live Action Short Film, with 'The Singers' and 'Two People Exchanging Saliva' both winning.

title: Oscars red carpet: See the best Academy Awards fashion
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stars including Kate Hudson, Timothee Chalamet, and Elle Fanning showcased top fashion at the 2026 Oscars red carpet.
Conan O'Brien returned as host for the second year, arriving at the Oscars held at Dolby Theatre, Los Angeles.
Other celebrities present included Leonardo DiCaprio, Steven Spielberg, and Alicia Silverstone.

title: All the way from Indonesia, she’s running the Boston Marathon for people with visual impairments
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas from Jakarta, Indonesia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support people with visual impairments.
Miraningtyas suffers from pterygium, a condition affecting vision, and aims to raise awareness and support for those needing eye surgery.
She emphasizes the importance of small actions in making a difference and hopes her run inspires support for vision-restoring treatments.

title: 5 takeaways from the Revolution’s 6-1 dismantling of FC Cincinnati
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Revolution defeated FC Cincinnati 6-1, tying a club record for goals in an MLS regular season match.
Brayan Ceballos scored twice from corner kicks; goals also by Dor Turgeman, Alhassan Yusuf, Griffin Yow, and Peyton Miller.
Luca Langoni provided three first-half assists, highlighting his influence on the game from the right wing position.

title: Man dies after kayak overturns off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 22-year-old man died after his kayak overturned in Cape Cod waters on Saturday, according to officials.
Local and federal emergency teams conducted an extensive search, deploying helicopters, drones, and multiple boats.
The man was found in critical condition and later pronounced dead at Cape Cod Hospital; his identity is withheld.

title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: St. Patrick's Day Parade in South Boston started from Andrew Square on March 15, 2026.
Mass. Gov. Maura Healey and Boston Mayor Michelle Wu began the parade by cutting a green ribbon.
Floats, dancers, and colorful attire highlighted the parade as spectators enjoyed the festive atmosphere.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: William Sarnie and Andre Silvia arrested for trafficking over 200 grams of cocaine after a fatal overdose investigation.
Silvia identified as main cocaine supplier, Sarnie linked through Barnstable police investigation; both face felony charges.
Investigation included local and state police, DEA, spanning multiple towns; arrests followed search warrants in Nantucket and Wareham.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and was the musical guest on the March 14, 2026 episode of 'Saturday Night Live.'
This was Styles' second time performing dual roles on 'SNL,' following his 2019 episode appearance.
Ryan Gosling introduced Styles' first performance, 'Dance No More,' from his album 'Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally.'

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation related to using Discord for sexualized interactions with minors.
Gavin pleaded guilty to coercion and possession of child porn, involving over 20 minors across multiple states and countries.
Investigators found 147 files of child sexual abuse material on Gavin's cellphone; he previously taught at Josiah Quincy Upper School.
